title: Files, Directories, Links, and Metadata · Command Retrieval
summary: Prepare a service workspace while preserving names, metadata, link intent, and the ability to reverse every operation. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a reversible filesystem-change plan and evidence tree after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
Use mkdir, touch, cp, mv, and rm with explicit targets, collision checks, and recovery-aware previews.
Predict inode, path, deletion, filesystem, and permission behavior for hard and symbolic links.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the File Types evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps File Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

Competency probe: Identify regular files, directories, symbolic links, devices, sockets, and pipes from metadata rather than filename appearance.
